claims.assertion: Salesforce total revenue for Q1 fiscal 2027 (three months ended April 30, 2026) was $11.1 billion, representing 13% year-over-year growth.
Subscription and support revenue grew 14% year-over-year to $10.593 billion in Q1 fiscal 2027.
Total remaining performance obligation as of April 30, 2026 was approximately $67.9 billion, an 11% increase year-over-year; current RPO was $33.6 billion, up 14% year-over-year.
Informatica contributed approximately $444 million of total revenues in Q1 fiscal 2027.
Data 360, Headless Platform, and Other subscription revenue grew 25% year-over-year to $3.683 billion in Q1 fiscal 2027, while Agentforce Apps grew only 9% to $6.910 billion.
Europe revenue grew 18% year-over-year to $2.754 billion in Q1 fiscal 2027, outpacing Americas (12%) and Asia Pacific (12%).
Salesforce's customer attrition rate as of April 30, 2026 was approximately 8%, excluding Slack self-service, Informatica, and current year acquisitions, and was consistent with the prior year.
Outstanding debt principal increased sharply from $14.5 billion as of January 31, 2026 to $39.5 billion as of April 30, 2026, primarily due to a $25 billion March 2026 notes issuance.
Salesforce describes itself as a global leader in customer relationship management (CRM) technology.

Revenue growth of 13% year-over-year is cited, but the revenue mix shift toward inorganic sources (Informatica contributing ~$444M) is not separated from organic growth in the headline figure, obscuring true organic growth rate.
Customer attrition rate improvement and retention metrics are cited without disclosure of cohort-level behavior, such as net revenue retention or expansion revenue by cohort vintage.

The Agentforce platform is described as enabling autonomous agents that reason, make decisions, and execute tasks, with no reference to third-party benchmarks or independent validation of agent performance.

Salesforce claims AI and agentic capabilities are integrated across all products ('every app'), but provides no disclosure of ML engineering headcount, patents, published research, or OSS contributions to substantiate AI differentiation.
Operating cash flow growth of 3% year-over-year significantly trails revenue growth of 13%, but no explanation of the margin compression in cash conversion is provided.
Foreign currency fluctuations positively impacted total revenues by approximately 2% year-over-year and current RPO growth by approximately 1% year-over-year in Q1 fiscal 2027.

Salesforce expects sales and marketing and general and administrative expenses to decrease as a percentage of revenues over time through self-serve, partner-led channels, AI, and agents, but provides no quantitative targets or timeline.

ddc32625-0079-4c63-a04e-94c66091af59.reasoning: The claim is fundamentally about a disclosure gap — specifically, whether cohort-level metrics such as NRR or expansion revenue by vintage are absent from reported retention narratives. None of the available signals (aggregate revenue, revenue growth rate, job postings, or GitHub commits) speak to the presence or absence of such disclosures in investor communications. The most relevant evidence for this claim would be a direct review of earnings transcripts, 10-K/10-Q filings, or investor day materials to check for cohort-level retention disclosures — none of which are surfaced here. This claim is structural and meta in nature (evaluating what a company chooses to report), making it effectively not testable with the signals provided.

bb8c8355-c1e3-412c-bd5c-9cbae93d6b73.reasoning: None of the four available signals speak directly to Informatica's segment-level revenue contribution in Q1 FY2027. The Tier 1 EDGAR signals reflect Salesforce's consolidated annual financials for FY2026 — a different entity, scope, and time period — while the Tier 2 signals (job postings, GitHub commits) are operational proxies with no causal link to quarterly revenue figures. The most important gap here is the absence of a segment-level revenue disclosure from Salesforce's Q1 FY2027 earnings filing or press release, which would be the definitive source. To evaluate this claim properly, one would need Salesforce's 10-Q or earnings supplement for Q1 FY2027 explicitly breaking out the Informatica contribution.

6ac58739-30da-418b-bab1-46343d765319.reasoning: None of the four signals directly address Europe segment revenue in Q1 fiscal 2027, which is the precise claim being evaluated. The most relevant signal — full-year company-wide revenue growth of 9.58% — covers a different time period and a different scope (consolidated vs. regional segment), so while the divergence from the claimed 18% European growth is notable, it cannot be treated as a direct contradiction of a segment-specific quarterly figure. To verify this claim, Q1 FY2027 segment-level revenue disclosures (e.g., 10-Q filings breaking out Americas, Europe, and Asia Pacific) would be required. The absence of any Tier 1 segment data for the relevant quarter makes this claim untestable with the current evidence set.

a5d80b4b-bb84-48d0-ba93-627febe87d53.reasoning: No signal directly covers the claimed period — Q1 FY2027 (three months ended April 30, 2026). The only financially relevant Tier 1 signal is full-year FY2026 annual revenue and growth (9.58% YoY), which neither confirms nor denies the quarterly claim and in fact shows a significantly lower growth rate than the asserted 13%, introducing mild skepticism. The two Tier 2 signals (job postings, GitHub commits) are entirely tangential to a specific quarterly revenue figure. To assess this claim properly, the definitive evidence needed would be Salesforce's Q1 FY2027 earnings release or 10-Q filing with EDGAR — which does not appear to be present in the signal set, likely because it had not yet been filed as of the data collection date.

8bf8121b-f42c-44da-8644-d2bed32d0644.reasoning: None of the four signals address the specific claim: the quantitative FX impact (~2% on total revenues, ~1% on current RPO) in Q1 fiscal 2027. The two Tier 1 EDGAR signals cover annual FY2026 financials and do not decompose currency effects, while the Tier 2 signals (job postings, GitHub commits) are entirely orthogonal to FX translation accounting. To validate this claim, one would need Q1 FY2027 earnings disclosures — specifically the constant-currency revenue and RPO disclosures versus reported figures, or an 8-K/10-Q filing for the quarter in question — none of which are present in the evidence set. The claim is precise and in principle testable, but the available signals simply cannot speak to it.

84208821-48d1-47eb-a90f-2c2b74fe3357.reasoning: None of the four available signals can address the specific claim, which requires Q1 fiscal 2027 segment-level subscription revenue figures for two distinct product categories. The only Tier 1 signals cover total company annual revenue for FY2026 — the wrong period, wrong granularity, and wrong fiscal quarter. The Tier 2 signals (job postings, GitHub commits) are useful for general business health proxies but are wholly unsuitable for validating precise dollar figures and growth rates at the sub-segment level. To properly evaluate this claim, one would need Salesforce's Q1 FY2027 10-Q or earnings supplement disclosing segment revenue breakdowns — data that either does not yet exist in the public record or was not retrieved here.

ce053daa-5ca0-410e-966b-6dd3742a9b8a.reasoning: None of the four available signals directly speak to the claimed Q1 fiscal 2027 subscription and support revenue of $10.593B at 14% YoY growth. The most relevant Tier 1 EDGAR signals cover total annual revenue through January 2026 — a different period, scope, and revenue line — and reflect a lower blended growth rate (~9.6%) that cannot be cleanly extrapolated to a specific segment in a later quarter. The key gap is the absence of a Q1 FY2027 quarterly earnings filing or press release (e.g., an EDGAR 10-Q for the quarter ending ~April 2026) that would disclose segment-level revenue. To verify this claim, a Tier 1 filing breaking out subscription and support revenue for the relevant quarter would be necessary and decisive.

f0a8ea5c-e550-4173-b4bc-389fcefc1a71.reasoning: The most critical finding is that the claim's stated revenue growth rate of 13% is directly contradicted by Tier 1 EDGAR XBRL data showing actual YoY revenue growth of 9.58% — a meaningful factual error that undermines the entire comparative framing. None of the available signals include operating cash flow figures, so the 3% OCF growth assertion and the implied cash conversion gap cannot be tested at all with the current evidence set. To properly evaluate this claim, prior-year operating cash flow (from the cash flow statement) and current-year OCF would be required, alongside a YoY gross margin comparison. As it stands, the factual premise of the claim is partially falsified, and the core analytical assertion (OCF divergence) is unverifiable with available signals.

982fc2f5-a6d9-4872-82ee-f7912dd45074.reasoning: None of the six available signals directly measure or proxy customer attrition — all are financial performance metrics (revenue, margins, EPS, operating income) or filing counts that are structurally disconnected from a retention rate claim. While Salesforce's healthy revenue growth (~9.6% YoY) and strong margins are broadly consistent with a company retaining the majority of its customer base, they cannot corroborate a precise figure of ~8% attrition or confirm year-over-year consistency. The claim is also forward-dated to April 30, 2026, which falls outside the period covered by the available FY2026 EDGAR filings (ending January 31, 2026). To evaluate this claim, analysts would need Salesforce's disclosed attrition or retention rate disclosures (typically found in 10-K/10-Q management commentary or supplemental data packages), ideally for both the current and prior fiscal period.

1a1356e3-47a5-4c41-944c-c632ccf83140.reasoning: The Tier 1 EDGAR signals establish that the company (consistent with Salesforce's scale and trajectory) generated ~$41.5B in revenue with ~9.6% YoY growth through January 2026, which is broadly compatible with $67.9B in total RPO and the claimed growth rates — RPO of ~1.6x trailing revenue is a normal range for a mature enterprise SaaS firm, and RPO/cRPO growth rates in the 11–14% range are plausible given revenue growth near 10%. However, the specific RPO figures ($67.9B total, $33.6B current) as of April 30, 2026 would ordinarily be disclosed in a Q1 FY2027 earnings release or 10-Q filing, and no such direct Tier 1 signal (e.g., an XBRL filing for the quarter ending April 30, 2026) is present in the evidence set. The most meaningful gap is the absence of the actual Q1 FY2027 RPO disclosure itself; confirmation would require the 10-Q or earnings supplement for that period. The Tier 2 signals (job postings, GitHub commits) add no probative value for this specific quantitative claim.

3a20fd02-e43a-4114-aa6e-f13a4dac1a4f.reasoning: None of the six signals directly address the claim's core assertions: the $14.5B outstanding principal as of January 31, 2026, the $39.5B balance as of April 30, 2026, or the $25B March 2026 notes issuance. All available signals are income-statement and earnings metrics (revenue, margins, EPS, operating income), which are structurally incapable of confirming balance sheet debt positions. The 8-K count of 12 is the only signal that could conceivably be proximate — a large debt issuance would typically trigger an 8-K filing — but the content of those filings is not provided, making this signal ambiguous at best. To properly evaluate this claim, the required evidence would include EDGAR balance sheet filings (10-Q as of April 30, 2026 disclosing long-term debt), a prospectus or 424B filing for the March 2026 notes, or an 8-K with the specific issuance details — none of which are present here.

5a292865-92db-49d3-8740-984bd2f0296e.reasoning: The available evidence confirms Salesforce is operating at scale with meaningful operating leverage — an ~20% operating margin in FY2026 versus historical low-single-digit margins is broadly consistent with the directional claim that expense ratios are declining. However, none of the signals directly measure S&M or G&A as a percentage of revenues over time, nor do they speak to the attribution mechanisms cited (self-serve, AI, agents, partner channels). The most relevant signal is operating income, which implies opex compression has already materially occurred, lending credibility to the forward-looking narrative. To meaningfully test the claim, one would need multi-year XBRL breakouts of S&M and G&A as explicit line items, ideally alongside management commentary quantifying channel mix shifts — none of which are present in these signals.

bde3e717-c755-440d-a635-3c41e4acbad9.reasoning: The Tier 1 EDGAR financials provide the strongest signal: the audited YoY revenue growth rate of ~9.6% diverges from the 13% headline cited in the claim, which itself validates the core assertion that reported growth figures may be selectively framed or period-specific, making organic vs. inorganic disaggregation harder to assess from top-line numbers alone. The ~$41.5B revenue base confirms that a ~$444M Informatica contribution (~1.1% of revenue) would be material enough to meaningfully distort organic growth if not separately disclosed. However, the claim cannot be fully supported because no signal directly confirms whether Informatica's contribution was or was not disclosed separately in segment or acquisition reporting, and the 13% figure itself is unverified against the EDGAR data — a key gap. To increase confidence, acquisition-specific revenue disclosures or management commentary separating organic growth from M&A contribution would be required.

7df0421b-2f49-4f28-9576-ac117ea03c37.reasoning: The claim is an implicit assertion about *what is absent* from Agentforce's marketing narrative — specifically, the lack of third-party benchmarks or independent performance validation for its autonomous agents. claim_drift.headline: Salesforce's Q1 FY2027 MD&A reflects a materially more aggressive posture: revenue growth accelerated to 13% on the Informatica acquisition, debt ballooned ~4× to fund a $25B accelerated buyback, the product taxonomy was overhauled to center on Agentforce, and macro/tariff risk language was quietly dropped while FX tailwinds replaced headwinds.

items.label: Revenue growth rate
items.then: Revenue of $9.8B, up 8% year-over-year for Q1 FY2026.
items.now: Revenue of $11.1B, up 13% year-over-year for Q1 FY2027.
items.significance: The acceleration from 8% to 13% growth is a material step-up; management attributes the bulk of incremental growth to Informatica ($444M contribution), making it important for analysts to assess organic vs. inorganic trajectory.
items.quote: For the three months ended April 30, 2026, revenue was $11.1 billion, an increase of 13 percent year-over-year.

items.label: Foreign currency impact on revenue
items.then: Total revenues were minimally impacted by foreign currency fluctuations in Q1 FY2026.
items.now: Total revenues in Q1 FY2027 were positively impacted by approximately two percent from foreign currency fluctuations.
items.significance: A swing from neutral/minimal FX to a ~2% tailwind materially flatters the reported 13% headline growth; on a constant-currency basis organic growth would be closer to 11%, a key distinction for analysts modeling underlying demand.
items.quote: Total revenues in the three months ended April 30, 2026 were positively impacted by approximately two percent in foreign currency fluctuations compared to the three months ended April 30, 2025.

items.label: Macro/tariff uncertainty risk
items.then: Prior filing explicitly warned: 'Unpredictability around economic policies or international trade, including tariffs, could result in consumer and economic uncertainty which may lead to slower growth in new and renewal business, potentially impacting our financial results.'
items.significance: The complete removal of the tariff and macro-uncertainty caution language, despite no resolution in the macro environment, signals a notable shift in management's risk narrative and may indicate higher confidence in demand or a desire to project resilience.
items.quote: Unpredictability around economic policies or international trade, including tariffs, could result in consumer and economic uncertainty which may lead to slower growth in new and renewal business, potentially impacting our financial results.

items.label: Debt load – massive increase via $25B Senior Notes + $6B term loan
items.then: As of April 30, 2025, principal due on outstanding debt was $8.5B; no term loans outstanding.
items.now: As of April 30, 2026, principal due on outstanding debt is $39.5B, including $25B in March 2026 Senior Notes and a $6B term loan.
items.significance: Debt outstanding increased roughly 4.6×, a landmark capital structure shift driven by the leveraged buyback; interest expense jumped to $317M vs. $68M, directly compressing free cash flow and introducing meaningful new financial risk.
items.quote: In March 2026, we also issued unsecured Senior Notes with an aggregate principal of $25.0 billion... We used the net proceeds from the March 2026 Notes to fund an accelerated share repurchase program.

items.label: $25B Accelerated Share Repurchase (ASR)
items.then: Prior filing reported a regular open-market repurchase of ~10M shares for ~$2.7B in Q1 FY2026; total board authorization was $30B aggregate.
items.now: In March 2026, Salesforce executed a $25B ASR, initially repurchasing ~103M shares at ~$198/share; board authorization increased to $50B, replacing the prior authorization.
items.significance: The ASR is one of the largest in US corporate history and represents a fundamental change in capital allocation strategy, funded by debt issuance, which analysts must factor into diluted share count, leverage, and sustainability of capital returns.
items.quote: In March 2026, we entered into the ASR Agreements with a syndicate of financial institutions to repurchase an aggregate of $25.0 billion of our common stock and received an initial delivery of approximately 103 million shares.

items.label: Product taxonomy overhauled to Agentforce Apps vs. Data 360/Headless Platform
items.then: Revenue disaggregated into five offerings: Sales (7% growth), Service (7%), Platform & Other (14%), Marketing & Commerce (3%), Integration & Analytics (10%).
items.now: Revenue disaggregated into two categories: Agentforce Apps (65% of sub revenue, 9% growth) and Data 360, Headless Platform & Other (35%, 25% growth); prior five-category breakout eliminated.
items.significance: The recast makes year-over-year comparisons impossible on a like-for-like basis and reframes the company's identity squarely around Agentforce/AI, while also obscuring individual product-line performance that analysts previously tracked.
items.quote: Effective as of the first quarter of fiscal year 2027, we have revised the presentation of our disaggregated revenue disclosures to reflect the evolution of our product architecture to deliver the Agentic Enterprise.

items.label: Restructuring future cash obligations
items.then: As of April 30, 2025, expected approximately $160M–$190M in future cash payments related to restructuring.
items.now: As of April 30, 2026, expected approximately $130M–$150M in future cash payments related to restructuring.
items.significance: While the reduction suggests restructuring is winding down, the current quarter's restructuring charge doubled to $80M (from $36M), indicating ongoing workforce and data-center actions that belie a clean conclusion.
items.quote: As of April 30, 2026, we expect approximately $130 million to $150 million in future cash payments related to our restructuring initiatives.

items.label: Agentforce / agentic AI platform positioning
items.then: Agentforce described as 'a new layer of our trusted platform that enables companies to build and deploy AI agents'; introduced in Q3 FY2025, momentum noted in Q1 FY2026.
items.now: Agentforce repositioned as the core identity of the company: 'Salesforce is the platform that powers how humans and agents work together'; company mission reframed as helping organizations 'become agentic enterprises'; Agentforce embedded in every app and given top-level product-taxonomy status.
items.significance: The shift from Agentforce as a new product feature to the defining architecture of the entire company signals a major strategic pivot that underpins pricing, go-to-market, and M&A strategy (Informatica, Qualified, Regrello), and is the central thesis management is asking investors to underwrite.
items.quote: Salesforce is the platform that powers how humans and agents work together, whether using Customer 360 apps, Slack, Headless 360, or other user interfaces.
